howItWorks.intro: This closing cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Closing costs typically 2-6% of loan amount
Includes: origination, appraisal, title insurance, escrow, recording fees
Buyer pays most fees; seller typically pays agent commissions
howItWorks.outro: Real estate values and regulations vary significantly by location. Work with a local agent or attorney.

faq.question: How much are closing costs?
faq.answer: Average closing costs are 3-4% of the loan amount. On a $350K home with 20% down ($280K loan), expect $8,400-11,200. This includes lender fees, title insurance, appraisal, escrow, and government recording fees.

faq.question: Can I negotiate closing costs?
faq.answer: Yes. You can negotiate lender fees, shop for title insurance (savings of $500-1,000), ask the seller for a credit (common in buyer's markets), and compare loan estimates from multiple lenders. Always negotiate.

faq.question: What is included in closing costs?
faq.answer: Typical breakdown: loan origination (0.5-1%), appraisal ($400-600), title insurance ($1,000-2,000), escrow/attorney ($500-1,500), recording fees ($100-300), home inspection ($300-500), and prepaid taxes/insurance.

faq.question: Is the Closing Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Closing Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Closing Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your home price, down payment, state cost level, you get instant results including estimated closing costs, total cash needed, major components. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated closing costs right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your home price and need to find the right estimated closing costs. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your home price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both estimated closing costs and total cash needed — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your state cost level, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
